mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and rising corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as investors price in stronger economic fundamentals.
failure_condition: If INDPRO is above its 12-month moving average but forward 60-day S&P 500 returns are negative in more than half of instances over a 5-year walk-forward window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate falling below 5% signals a tight labor market that compresses corporate margins through rising unit labor costs and triggers preemptive Fed tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return when UNRATE is below 5% is not significantly negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year period, this hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated housing starts and retail sales indicate overheating consumer demand that will draw monetary tightening and increase recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as forward guidance shifts hawkish.
failure_condition: If the joint HOUST > 2000 & RSAFS > 350000 regime does not produce negative average 60-day returns with a Sharpe ratio below the unconditional mean, the interaction hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate path during a tightening cycle raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and increases corporate borrowing costs, causing the S&P 500 to decline over the subsequent 20 trading days as valuations compress.
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day return conditional on a rising FEDFUNDS 3-month slope is not negative on average (rolling 2-year mean rank IC < 0), the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP signals broad economic growth that boosts corporate revenues and profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings revisions climb and risk appetite increases.
failure_condition: If the directional relationship between GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is negative or random (Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding the Fed's implicit target forces a tighter monetary stance than anticipated, increasing uncertainty and raising real discount rates, which depresses S&P 500 prices over the following month.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward S&P 500 return in months when CPI exceeds 2.5% YoY is not negative in at least 55% of observations over a 10-year walk-forward, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with robust retail sales signals an economy near full capacity, where any further acceleration forces the Fed into restrictive policy, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as tightening fears outweigh immediate growth optimism.
failure_condition: If the joint signal from PAYEMS and RSAFS does not produce a negative average 20-day forward return with a t-statistic below -1.5 over a 5-year period, the interaction mechanism is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: After an extended period of robust job creation, a deceleration in monthly payroll growth below 100,000 indicates the labor market is rolling over, foreshadowing a recession that will erode corporate earnings and drive the S&P 500 lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the 60-day S&P 500 return after a PAYEMS print below +100k is not negative in at least 60% of occurrences over a 10-year period, the regime-conditional hypothesis is falsified.
